name: Shrimp Won Ton
recipeIngredient: 3/4 lb Medium shrimp (25 to 35 per pound), shelled and deveined
1 tablespoon Dry sherry or rice wine
4 Water chestnuts, peeled and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Coarsely chop shrimp into small pea-sized morsels.
2. Toss with 1/2 teaspoon salt; set aside for 10 minutes to season.
3. In a large bowl, combine pork, ginger, sugar, white pepper, soy sauce, sherry or rice wine, sesame oil, cornstarch, and green onion mixture. Add the shrimp; mix well.
4. Work with one won ton at a time; keep remaining skins covered with a damp towel.
5. Place a wrapper flat in your palm with a corner pointing away from you and a corner pointing at you. Put about 1 heaping teaspoon of filling in the top corner (a little way down from the point).
6. Fold the point toward you over the filling, tucking in the filling, then roll the corner over toward the center of the wrapper.
7. The won ton should look like a triangle. Pinch around the filling to contain it.
8. Using both hands, grasp the two opposite corners of the won ton and pull them up over the filling, overlapping slightly.
9. Moisten the overlapped edges with water; pinch to seal.
10. Set the wrapped won tons on a baking sheet and cover with a cloth.
11. Repeat steps 4-10 with remaining ingredients.
12. The dumplings are ready for boiling or can be frozen for several months. Makes about 5 dozen won tons.
